advertSummary: Calisen is seeking a Portfolio and Strategy Lead in Manchester on a fixed-term contract until the end of 2026, with potential to become permanent. The role combines strategic portfolio management, PMO discipline, governance, reporting, and senior stakeholder support, with a focus on prioritisation, delivery performance, and continuous improvement.

coreResponsibilities: Develop and maintain portfolio governance frameworks

rawAdvertText:
Portfolio and Strategy Lead - Manchester M2 1HW - Indeed.com 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home Company reviews Salary guide Sign in Sign in 1 new update Employers / Post Job Start of main content 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 What Where Find Jobs Portfolio and Strategy Lead CAML 4.0 4.0 out of 5 stars Manchester M2 1HW £60,000 - £70,000 a year - Full-time CAML 2 reviews Read what people are saying about working here. Job details Pay £60,000 - £70,000 a year Job type Full-time &nbsp; Location Manchester M2 1HW &nbsp; Benefits Pulled from the full job description Annual leave Employee discount Sick pay Company pension Private medical insurance Car scheme &nbsp; Full job description 
 Join Calisen as our Portfolio and Strategy Lead! We’re looking for a strategic, delivery‑focused leader to sit at the heart of our transformation agenda. If you thrive at the intersection of strategy, governance, and change delivery - and you’re motivated by shaping how an organisation prioritises, governs, and executes its most important work - this role offers the perfect platform. 
 This is a fixed term contract until the end of 2026 - with the potential to be made permanent. 
 About the Role 
 As Portfolio and Strategy Lead, you will ensure our portfolio of programmes and projects is coherent, prioritised, and aligned with Calisen’s strategic objectives. You will combine hands‑on PMO discipline with strategic insight, enabling effective decision‑making across the organisation. 
 This role defines and operates our portfolio governance framework, ensuring transparency of delivery performance and providing senior stakeholders with accurate, timely information to support prioritisation and resourcing decisions. You’ll champion best practice in change delivery and drive continuous improvement across the Calisen group. 
 This is portfolio management in action - practical, influential, and central to how we deliver our strategic goals. 
 Key Responsibilities 
 
 Develop and maintain strong portfolio governance frameworks aligned to organisational strategy. 
 Ensure programmes and projects are prioritised based on strategic value, risk, capacity, and expected benefits. 
 Drive structured portfolio reviews, providing dashboards and insights to support executive decision-making. 
 Manage portfolio KPIs, standards, and controls, including spend tracking, delivery forecasting, and benefits realisation. 
 Deliver accurate reporting on project and programme status, risks, dependencies, and benefits. 
 Manage C‑Suite and Director expectations through clear, consistent communication. 
 Lead Centralised Best Practice - own the Change Delivery Framework and drive continuous improvement. 
 Own and manage the resource allocation process for priority initiatives. 
 
 About You 
 You’ll bring a blend of strategic thinking, analytical capability, and hands‑on portfolio delivery. 
 Qualifications 
 
 Degree in a relevant discipline 
 Professional project/programme management accreditation e.g. PRINCE2, MSP, MoP 
 
 Knowledge 
 
 Strong understanding of portfolio management methodologies (e.g., MoP) 
 Knowledge of project/programme methodologies (PRINCE2, Agile) 
 Confident with data, reporting, Power BI, dashboards, and KPIs 
 
 Experience 
 
 Extensive hands‑on experience managing strategic, enterprise‑wide portfolios 
 Demonstrated ability to establish and lead Portfolio Office functions 
 Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills 
 Strong senior‑level influencing capability 
 Ability to analyse complex portfolios and support effective decision‑making 
 Experience in the utilities or energy sector (Desirable) 
 
 Technical Competencies 
 
 Advanced portfolio management skills - prioritisation, dependency mapping, governance 
 Ability to lead organisations through adoption of portfolio management 
 Strong strategic alignment and benefits realisation capability 
 Resource and capacity planning across enterprise portfolios 
 Financial management experience: budgeting, forecasting, cost control 
 Strong change management advocacy 
 Able to analyse delivery risk, highlight trends, and focus stakeholders on key issues 
 Comfortable operating between strategic (Exec‑level) and hands‑on delivery work 
 
 
 Benefits 
 
 Annual bonus, based on company and personal performance 
 Life assurance at 4x basic salary 
 Pension scheme - Calisen will double your contribution up to 7% 
 Electric Vehicle Salary Sacrifice Scheme 
 Private Medical Insurance 
 Company sick pay 
 24/7 Employee Assistance Programme 
 25 days annual leave, increasing annually up to 30 days, plus 8 bank holidays 
 Your birthday off 
 Enhanced maternity and paternity pay 
 Access to the My Rewards Platform, offering discounts at hundreds of retailers 
 
 
 Please note: All successful candidates must pass a criminal record check, provide us with 5 years’ work references, and prove their eligibility to work in the UK. 
 
 IND789

(Key concepts and benefits explained) What is the AIDA model and how does it help sales? &nbsp; Similar jobs nearby Shift Leader jobs in Manchester &nbsp;

cvText:
Ben T. Howard | Senior Operations and Transformation Leader 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Ben Howard 
 Senior Operations and Transformation Leader

 
 

 
 Professional Profile 
 Senior operations and transformation leader with experience across NHS primary care, specialist manufacturing, leisure operations and regulated data services. Delivers operational turnaround, service redesign, financial control and performance improvement in complex, fast-moving environments. Combines strategic oversight with hands-on delivery across governance, workforce planning, reporting and service execution. Known for bringing structure, commercial grip and measurable results.

 

 
 Professional Experience 

 
 
 
 
 Operations, Development and Transformation Manager 
 NHS Primary Care

 
 2024 - Present

 
 
 Leading operational performance, transformation and service control across clinical, administrative and patient-facing functions, supporting delivery across a £1.56m annual income base. 
 Improved patient experience by increasing Google review score from 2.09 to 4.51 and raising five-star reviews from 19.8% to 81.3% across 209 reviews received during this period. 
 Supported a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation, released £33.3k of working capital through a 40.3% reduction in debtor balances and helped increase cash at bank by £172.6k year on year. 
 Built senior reporting across workflow, room utilisation, payroll and contractual income, including reconciliation of 64 PCSE remittance files covering £2.83m. 
 
 

 
 
 
 Operations Manager 
 Copper Alloys Ltd

 
 2022 - 2024

 
 
 Led day-to-day operations within a specialist manufacturing environment during a scale-up phase, supporting disciplined delivery across production, people, systems and customer requirements. 
 Contributed to operational improvements during a period in which gross profit margin increased from 29% to 39% and orders won rose by 60%. 
 Improved operational resilience through changes to processes, equipment, capacity and ways of working, supporting business growth in a precision manufacturing setting. 
 Used sales, delivery and KPI reporting to monitor performance, maintain customer service levels and strengthen execution across the operation. 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 Contact 
 
 Phone 
 07507 789672 

 Email 
 Ben@CheckLoops.co.uk 

 Location 
Leek, Staffordshire

 
 

 
 Education 
 
 
 Executive MBA 
 Aston University

 2020 - 2023

 
 
 Level 5 Apprenticeship, Operations Departmental Manager 
 Staffordshire University

 2018 - 2020

 
 
 Level 5 Diploma in Organisational Leadership 
 Institute of Leadership and Management

 2018 - 2020

 
 
 

 
 Core Skills 
 
 Operational Performance 
 Service Improvement and Redesign 
 KPI Governance and Reporting 
 Financial Control and Budget Oversight 
 Patient Access and Service Delivery 
 Workforce Utilisation and Planning 
 Change Delivery and Stakeholder Engagement 
 Power BI, EMIS, Accurx 
 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 Professional Experience 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 Operations & Continuous Improvement Manager 
 Merlin Entertainments | Alton Towers Resort

 
 2016 - 2022

 
 
 Progressed through operational and continuous improvement roles, combining frontline resort operations with cross-site transformation delivery across five major European theme park sites. 
 Managed live operations across major attraction launches and seasonal events, including the opening of Wicker Man, a £16m investment and the UK's first new wooden rollercoaster in over 20 years. 
 Led cross-site improvement activity across Alton Towers, Chessington, Gardaland, Heide Park and Thorpe Park, using operational data and frontline insight to strengthen operating standards. 
 Coordinated live operations across teams of more than 300 in a KPI-led guest-experience environment, using live feedback tools and digital ride-time information to support operational decision-making. 
 
 

 
 
 
 Product Executive 
 Experian

 
 2010 - 2016

 
 
 Supported business-critical delivery and client service within a regulated, data-sensitive corporate environment. 
 Coordinated workload allocation and training across a 12-person function to maintain service standards and processing performance. 
 Built early capability in stakeholder management, process control and performance-led delivery within a structured corporate setting. 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 Sector Experience 
 
 NHS and healthcare operations 
 Specialist manufacturing 
 Leisure, attractions and visitor operations 
 Regulated data and corporate services 
 
 

 
 Leadership Themes 
 
 Operational turnaround and stabilisation 
 Service redesign and customer experience improvement 
 Commercial grip, reporting and financial control 
 Cross-functional leadership in complex environments

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Portfolio control, clear reporting, and practical change leadership.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I bring a mix of portfolio discipline, operational control, and hands-on change delivery that fits the shape of this role. My background is in environments where priorities shift, data is imperfect, and senior people need clear answers fast.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: Calisen is asking for someone who can sit close to transformation, governance, and delivery performance, and that is the kind of environment I work well in. I also notice the role is framed as a fixed-term appointment with potential to become permanent, which suggests Calisen wants someone who can make a visible impact quickly and build trust with senior stakeholders.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: The work itself is close to what I do best: bringing structure to complex activity, improving visibility, and helping leaders make better prioritisation decisions. I would expect to add value where portfolio coherence, reporting discipline, and resource allocation all need to work together rather than sit in separate silos.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: data, reporting, dashboards, and executive insight
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I chose this because I have built practical reporting that helps leaders see pressure points quickly and act on them. That is directly relevant where portfolio decisions depend on clear dashboards and timely insight.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting proof point for reporting, KPI thinking, and decision support.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve built reporting that turns fragmented operational data into something leaders can actually use.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: portfolio prioritisation, governance, and resource control
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I chose this because I’ve worked in a scale-up environment where delivery discipline, capacity, and operational resilience all mattered at once. That is a good match for a role that has to balance priorities, resources, and performance.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as evidence of operating in a fast-moving, high-control environment.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve worked where growth only held together if priorities, capacity, and execution stayed tight.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: continuous improvement and measurable performance improvement
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I chose this because it shows I can improve outcomes in a live service environment and sustain the change through better control and follow-through. That matters where the role is expected to improve transparency and delivery performance across a portfolio.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a results-led example of improvement under pressure.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve turned around performance by tightening control around the things that affect outcomes most.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I read this as a role that needs more than portfolio administration. It needs someone who can impose order on competing priorities, give senior leaders reliable information, and keep governance useful rather than bureaucratic. It also needs someone who is comfortable moving between detail and executive conversation.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led operations in a growth phase where capacity, process discipline, and day-to-day decisions all had to be managed carefully.
experienceMappings.relevance: That experience translates well to prioritising work by value, risk, and capacity rather than by noise.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve built senior reporting across workflow, room utilisation, payroll, and contractual income, and I’ve used Power BI in my core skills set.
experienceMappings.relevance: This supports the need for clear portfolio reporting, KPI tracking, and decision-ready information.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: In NHS primary care I improved patient experience and strengthened operational grip around service follow-through.
experienceMappings.relevance: That shows I can take a live environment, improve performance, and keep the change practical.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: At Merlin I worked across live operations and cross-site improvement activity, where clear coordination and fast decisions mattered.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is relevant to managing Director and C-Suite expectations in a busy portfolio setting.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in environments where production, people, and operational resilience all had to be balanced while the business grew.
experienceMappings.relevance: That gives me a grounded view of capacity planning and the trade-offs behind allocation decisions.

focusAreasToBring.title: Portfolio visibility
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on making the status of work, risks, dependencies, and benefits easier to see and easier to challenge.

focusAreasToBring.title: Practical governance
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep governance tight enough to support decisions, but simple enough that it helps delivery rather than slowing it down.

focusAreasToBring.title: Resource discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would look closely at where capacity is stretched, where priorities conflict, and how to make allocation decisions more defensible.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background lines up well with a role that sits between strategy, control, and delivery. I’ve worked in regulated, operationally demanding environments where reporting, stakeholder confidence, and measurable improvement all mattered. I also bring the kind of hands-on style that makes portfolio management useful to senior leaders rather than just well documented.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first months tightening visibility, clarifying priorities, and making portfolio reporting more decision-ready. I would also look to strengthen the change delivery framework, improve the quality of governance conversations, and help leaders see where capacity or dependency issues are likely to affect delivery.
companyHighlights: Calisen is hiring for a strategic portfolio leadership role focused on transformation, governance, and change delivery.
The role is fixed term until the end of 2026, with potential to become permanent.
The brief places this role close to senior stakeholders and executive decision-making.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments that value clear communication, transparency, and practical improvement. That matches the tone of the brief, which feels delivery-focused and interested in best practice without losing sight of what actually gets done.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the current portfolio, the governance rhythm, the reporting pack, and the main decision points so I can see where clarity is strong and where it is missing.
I would focus on prioritisation logic, dashboard quality, dependency visibility, and the way risks and benefits are being presented to senior stakeholders.
I would look to strengthen the change delivery framework, make resource allocation more consistent, and identify a few practical improvements that make governance easier to use.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring structured portfolio thinking, clear reporting, and a practical approach to change that helps Calisen make better decisions and deliver with more confidence.
closingProofPoints: I improved Google reviews from 2.09 to 4.51 in NHS primary care.
I built senior reporting across workflow, utilisation, payroll, and income.
I’ve led operations through growth, improvement, and tighter performance control.
contentNotes: I have not claimed direct utilities or energy sector experience because it is not supported by the CV or evidence bank.
I have not stated formal PRINCE2/MSP/MoP accreditation because it is not shown in the CV context provided.
Evidence selection favours reporting, governance, and operational control because those are the strongest matches to the brief.
